features.featureTitle: In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx), update the brush/paint tool workflow so that: (1) The user activates the Paint tool button to enter paint mode and paints over the area of the image they want to remove (the selected area is highlighted with a semi-transparent red overlay as already implemented). (2) After painting the desired area, the user clicks a dedicated 'Erase' button (distinct from the existing erase-mode toggle) that processes the canvas and removes only the painted/selected pixels — making them transparent (PNG output) or replacing them with the chosen solid color (JPG output). (3) The 'Erase' button must be prominently visible in the BrushToolPanel or the main toolbar whenever there are painted brush strokes on the canvas (i.e., the mask is non-empty). (4) The existing 'Remove Background' flow in RemovalPanel.tsx must continue to work as before for auto-detect and full background removal. (5) The Erase button must be styled using the existing tool-btn and teal/cyan accent CSS classes consistent with the glassmorphism design system.
